meta.title: G15 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: BMW 8 Series G15 engine replacement costs — B58, N63, S63, B57 petrol and diesel. Real UK prices, V8 ownership costs, M8 maintenance, and grand tourer‑specific guidance from 20+ vetted BMW specialists.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 8 Series G15 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G15 engine typically costs £4,000–£16,000 supply only, depending on the engine — the B58 in the 840i sits at £4,000–£7,000, the B57 in the 840d is £5,000–£8,000, the N63 in the M850i is £7,000–£12,000, and the S63 in the M8/M8 Competition is £9,000–£16,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 8 Series G15 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30C in the 840i is the most reliable engine in the G15 range — BMW's modular straight-six is chain-driven, robust, and widely regarded as one of BMW's most dependable modern engines. On the diesel side, the B57D30B in the 840d xDrive is the most reliable option — 340 PS, proven reliability, and strong economy. The N63 V8 in the M850i and S63 in the M8 are the least reliable and most expensive to maintain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 8 Series G15?

acceptedAnswer.text: Valve stem seal failure on the N63 in the M850i xDrive and turbocharger/valve stem wear on the S63 in the M8/M8 Competition are the main engine issues. On the B58 in the 840i, cooling system maintenance (expansion tank/water pump) is the primary concern. On the 840d xDrive, the 48V mild-hybrid system is relatively new and may have integration issues. The G15's grand tourer layout doesn't affect engine reliability.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 8 Series G15 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The B58 straight-six in the 840i and B57 straight-six diesel in the 840d xDrive are well-regarded and proven. The N63 V8 in the M850i carries a significant risk of valve stem seal failure — budget for this repair. The S63 in the M8/M8 Competition is the most complex and expensive to maintain. As with any BMW, service history is everything.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 8 Series G15 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't say 'avoid' outright — but an N63-powered M850i without valve stem seal history is a significant gamble. An S63-powered M8 without documented maintenance history is even more of a risk. A well-maintained B58 840i or B57 840d is the safest choice. If you must have a V8, budget £3,000–£6,000 for valve stem seals at 50,000–80,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G15 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Blue smoke on startup (N63/S63 valve stem seals), excessive oil consumption (N63/S63), loss of power or turbo whine (S63), or coolant loss/overheating (B58) are the key warning signs. On the M850i and M8, oil consumption of more than 1 litre per 1,000 miles is a classic N63/S63 symptom. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 8 Series G15 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: As a premium grand tourer with values above £25,000, a £5,000–£10,000 engine replacement is 20–40% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Replacement is almost always financially viable. Extended warranty cover is strongly recommended, especially for V8 models and the 48V mild-hybrid system on the 840d.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 8 Series G15 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 six-cylinder can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — it's one of BMW's most durable modern engines. The B57 diesel six is similarly robust. The N63 V8 is the least durable — valve stem seal failure is common before 100,000 miles. The S63 in the M8 requires rigorous maintenance; with proper care, it can reach 150,000+ miles, but running costs are significant.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G15, G14, and G16?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G14 is the 8 Series convertible (2019–present), the G15 is the 8 Series coupé (2018–2026), and the G16 is the 8 Series Gran Coupé (2020–present). All three share the same platform and engine range. The G15 is the two-door coupé variant — this page covers the G15 specifically. See the G14 and G16 pages for convertible and Gran Coupé variants.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 8 Series G15 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block. Common G15 codes include B58B30C (840i), N63B44T3 (M850i xDrive), S63B44T4 (M8/M8 Competition), B57D30B (840d xDrive), and N63B44T3 (Alpina B8). Tell us your variant and registration date and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: BMW 8 Series G15 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the 8 Series Coupé G15 — from the 3.0-litre B58 straight-six in the 840i, to the 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8 in the M850i xDrive, and the fire-breathing S63 V8 in the M8 and M8 Competition. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the 2024 model year updates that keep BMW's flagship grand tourer relevant. Backed by 120+ real UK F06/F13 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 8 Series G15 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G15 Coupé — from the entry-level 840i to the range-topping M8 Competition, including the V8-powered M850i and the Alpina B8.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: The G15 uses BMW's established engine families — B58 (six-cylinder petrol), N63 (V8 petrol), S63 (M V8), and B57 (six-cylinder diesel). The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines — it's chain-driven, robust, and has few reported major failures. The N63 V8 has a well-earned reputation for higher ownership costs due to its hot-vee design. The S63 in the M8 is the most powerful and most expensive to maintain. The B57 diesel is proven and reliable. All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages and verified enquiry data for this specific model.

overview.intro: The BMW 8 Series G15 is the two-door coupé variant of the second-generation 8 Series, unveiled at the 24 Hours of Le Mans on 15 June 2018. It marks the return of the 8 Series nameplate after 19 years and serves as the successor to the BMW 6 Series (F06/F12/F13). Built on BMW's CLAR platform and manufactured in Dingolfing, Germany, the G15 is powered by a range of turbocharged six-cylinder petrol, six-cylinder diesel, and V8 petrol engines, all mated to an 8-speed ZF automatic transmission. The range includes the B58-powered 840i, the N63 V8-powered M850i xDrive, the B57 diesel-powered 840d xDrive, and the S63 V8-powered M8 and M8 Competition. Production is expected to continue through to 2026.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR platform • Predecessor: BMW 6 Series F06/F12/F13 • Successor: None (current generation) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G14 Convertible, G16 Gran Coupé.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The 8 Series G15 shares its engine line-up with the G14 and G16 — B58 enquiries totalled 540 and N63/S63 enquiries totalled 440/410 in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], with the M850i xDrive attracting the highest proportion of performance-oriented enquiries.

items.quote: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel produces 340 PS and 700 Nm — effortless, refined, and with 11 hp electric boost from the 48V mild-hybrid system. The B57 is chain-driven and proven across the BMW range. In the G15 coupé, it's the diesel you buy if you want six-cylinder refinement without V8 running costs.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless torque and 40+ mpg potential.

items.quote: The B58 is BMW's modular straight-six masterpiece — 340 PS, 450 Nm, silky smooth, and chain-driven with a robust bottom end. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. In the G15 coupé, it's the petrol you can buy without anxiety.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder performance without the V8's running costs. (Model-wide: B58B30 is the benchmark across the BMW range — the G15 offers the best expression of it in a grand tourer.)

items.quote: The hot-vee V8 suffers from heat-related failures — valve stem seals, turbo coolant lines, and battery drain are common. The N63 has a well-earned reputation for high ownership costs. The M850i is fast, but it's expensive to keep fast.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with deep pockets who want V8 performance and are prepared for the maintenance bills.

items.quote: The S63 is the high-performance version of the N63 — up to 625 PS, twin-turbo, and even more complex. Valve stem seal failure, turbocharger wear, and the M-specific cooling system all add to the ownership cost. A full engine replacement sits at £9,000–£16,000.
items.whoItsFor: M8 buyers who budget for high-end maintenance and have a specialist they trust.

items.quote: The 840i offers 340 PS, 450 Nm, and near-40 mpg potential — it's the most efficient petrol in the range and the cheapest to replace. The B58 is robust, the chain is accessible, and the G15's weight doesn't overwhelm it.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the best balance of performance, economy, and reliability.

items.quote: The 840d is often overshadowed by the V8 M850i and the M8, but the B57 straight-six diesel is the most efficient engine in the range — 340 PS, 700 Nm, and 44+ mpg potential. It's the grand tourer's grand tourer — effortless torque and real-world economy.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel refinement without the V8's running costs.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Valve stem seals / turbo coolant lines
rows.ownershipVerdict: Highest Risk — 530 PS, high ownership costs
Most Expensive — 625 PS, ultimate M costs

rows.commonMajorFailure: Valve stem seals / turbo coolant lines

economicsRule.title: The G15 rule
economicsRule.text: A £5,000–£8,000 B57 recon against a £25,000–£65,000 G15 sits at 8–32% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even the most expensive engine replacement (M8 Competition S63 at £10,000–£16,000) is only 15–64% of a well-kept G15's value. The G15 is a premium grand tourer with strong retained values — replacement is almost always financially viable. Extended warranty cover is still recommended, especially for V8 models and the 48V mild-hybrid system on the 840d.

problems.title: N63 Valve Stem Seal Failure (M850i xDrive)
problems.rootCause: The N63's hot-vee design (turbochargers located in the V of the engine) creates significant heat — valve stem seals can harden and fail, leading to oil consumption, blue smoke on startup, and eventually, catalytic converter damage. This is the defining issue of the N63 across all BMW models.
problems.recommendation: On any N63-powered G15, have the valve stem seals inspected at 50,000 miles — a £3,000–£6,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £7,000–£12,000 engine replacement that follows if ignored. This is the defining ownership cost of the M850i.

cta.label: Compare N63 engine prices for your G15 M850i xDrive →

problems.title: S63 Valve Stem Seal & Turbocharger Wear (M8 / M8 Competition)
problems.rootCause: The S63 shares the N63's hot-vee architecture but with higher outputs — valve stem seal failure is common, and the turbochargers can suffer from wear due to the increased boost pressures. The M-specific cooling system also requires specialist maintenance.
problems.recommendation: On any S63-powered M8, have the valve stem seals and turbochargers inspected at 50,000 miles — a £4,000–£7,000 preventative service is dramatically cheaper than the £9,000–£16,000 engine replacement that follows if ignored. The M8 is rewarding but requires serious maintenance commitment.

cta.label: Compare S63 engine prices for your G15 M8 →

problems.title: B58 Cooling System Maintenance (840i)
problems.rootCause: The B58's cooling system uses plastic components that can become brittle with age and heat cycling — the expansion tank and water pump are the most common failure points. The B58 is otherwise one of BMW's most reliable engines, but cooling system maintenance is essential.
problems.recommendation: On any B58-powered G15 over 60,000 miles, have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£800 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £1,000–£2,000 cooling system overhaul or engine replacement that follows an overheat event. The B58 is otherwise a robust engine.

cta.label: Compare B58 cooling system repair quotes for your G15 840i →

problems.title: 48V Mild-Hybrid System Integration (840d xDrive)
problems.rootCause: The 48V mild-hybrid system (integrated starter-generator, 48V battery) provides 11 hp of electric boost and smoother stop-start operation. Early issues may include battery degradation, software integration problems, or starter-generator failures.
problems.recommendation: As a relatively new system, long-term reliability data is limited. Ensure the 48V system is covered under warranty and have it inspected at major services. The mild-hybrid system provides efficiency benefits and smoother stop-start operation but adds complexity.

replacementCosts.h2: G15 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are G15 Coupé (two-door grand tourer) only. G14 Convertible and G16 Gran Coupé share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages. The 840i uses the B58B30C (340 PS). The M850i xDrive uses the N63B44T3 (530 PS). The M8 uses the S63B44T4 (600 PS) and the M8 Competition produces 625 PS. The 840d xDrive uses the B57D30B with 48V mild-hybrid (340 PS). The Alpina B8 uses a tuned N63 V8 producing 620 PS. The 840i and 840d are available with rear-wheel drive, while the M850i xDrive, M8, and M8 Competition are all-wheel drive (xDrive) only.
